title: coleus caninus subsp. flavovirens
intro: Coleus caninus subsp. flavovirens, also known as Coleus aromaticus, is a plant species that belongs to the family Lamiaceae and is native to the tropical regions of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits associated with Coleus caninus subsp. flavovirens include reducing inflammation, treating digestive issues, and providing relief from fever and respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being and health.
